_boundary_note: Dot-free per the 2026-05-29 finding: dotted names (e.g. 'test.eval') are format-rejected with -32602 BEFORE the auth gate. The spec §0 'test.eval' must become a dot-free authorized eval boundary. The harness seeds into whatever dot-free boundary is authorized in the MCP session.
_purpose: A7 over-abstention / recall-cliff calibration set. Live finding (2026-05-29): a zero-lexical-overlap paraphrase false-abstained on a fact the vault holds. memory_search showed the relevant fact retrieved at sem_rank=4, behind irrelevant semantically-adjacent facts at sem_rank 1-3; ADR-057 abstains on TOP-1 cosine, so an irrelevant sub-0.66 top-1 buries the rank-4 answer. These cases pin recall across paraphrase distance AND guard against over-correction (lowering the floor must not surface junk top-1 or break true-negative abstention).
_scoring: Per spec §6: seed each case, run query via memory_read, score relevant_facts against expect. Emit per-ability precision/recall + abstention confusion matrix (false-abstain vs false-answer). The must_rank_top_k checks in the rank-displacement cases specifically test whether the relevant fact reaches top-1, not just whether it is retrieved.

cases._targets: CONTROL (should already pass): query shares topical tokens ('development workflow') with the fact.

seed_memories.content: The user prefers per-action commit approvals and four definition-of-done gates (build, test, clippy, fmt) in their development workflow.
The user enjoys trail running in the foothills on weekends.

cases.query: what are the user's development workflow preferences?

cases._targets: THE CLIFF (currently fails): zero-token-overlap paraphrase of the same fact. This is the exact failure reproduced live.

seed_memories.content: The user prefers per-action commit approvals and four definition-of-done gates (build, test, clippy, fmt) in their development workflow.
The user enjoys trail running in the foothills on weekends.

cases.query: how does the user like to sign off on changes and verify things are solid before merging?

cases._targets: THE ROOT PATHOLOGY found live: the relevant fact must beat semantically-adjacent-but-irrelevant distractors (modeled on the 'read-after-write fix' and 'agents hand off correctness/quality' facts that out-ranked the real answer at sem_rank 1-2). must_rank_top_k k=1 forces the fix to put the answer at top-1, which is what the ADR-057 top-1 gate depends on.

seed_memories.content: The user prefers per-action commit approvals and four definition-of-done gates (build, test, clippy, fmt) in their development workflow.
The user spent the afternoon verifying that the read-after-write behaviour was fixed.
The user wrote a long note on how coding agents hand off correctness and quality context between sessions.

cases.query: how does the user like to sign off on changes and verify quality before shipping?

cases._targets: Graded paraphrase, MEDIUM distance, different fact type (tooling preference).

seed_memories.content: The user works primarily in a dark-themed editor and finds light themes straining.

cases.query: what kind of visual setup does the user use when coding?

cases._targets: Graded paraphrase, FAR distance, same fact as A7-editor-medium. Pair them to read off the recall cliff for this fact type.

seed_memories.content: The user works primarily in a dark-themed editor and finds light themes straining.

cases.query: is the user bothered by bright screens?

cases._targets: Paraphrase recall when the answer requires a fact written by a different agent, amid an unrelated fact. Checks the cliff is not an artifact of single-fact vaults.

seed_memories.content: The user relocated to Lisbon in March 2026 for a fresh start.
The user collects vintage mechanical keyboards.

cases.query: where is the user based these days?

cases._targets: SYNONYM GAP (the live 2026-06-01 bug): zero-token-overlap meaning leap food<->cuisine. memory_read 'what food does the user like?' false-abstained on an active 'favourite cuisine is Japanese' fact (rank-1 in memory_search). must_exclude is a topically-adjacent eating-habit near-miss that does NOT state a food preference.

seed_memories.content: The user's favourite cuisine is Japanese.
The user usually skips breakfast on weekdays.

cases.query: what food does the user like?

cases._targets: SYNONYM GAP: 'how does the user get to work?' <-> 'cycles to the office'. Tests the same class as food/cuisine on a different domain (transport). must_exclude is a commute-adjacent gripe that does NOT state the mode of travel.

seed_memories.content: The user cycles to the office most mornings.
The user finds the morning traffic on the bridge frustrating.

cases.query: how does the user get to work?

cases._targets: SYNONYM GAP: 'does the user have any pets?' <-> 'has a golden retriever named Biscuit' (pet<->dog/retriever, category<->instance). must_exclude is an animal-adjacent fact that does NOT imply pet ownership.

seed_memories.content: The user has a golden retriever named Biscuit.
The user volunteers at an animal shelter on Saturdays.

cases.query: does the user have any pets?

cases._targets: SYNONYM GAP: 'what does the user do for a living?' <-> 'works as a structural engineer' (for-a-living<->works-as, occupation idiom). must_exclude is a career-adjacent fact (a certification in progress) that is NOT the user's occupation.

seed_memories.content: The user works as a structural engineer.
The user is studying for a project management certification.

cases.query: what does the user do for a living?

cases._targets: SYNONYM GAP: 'what does the user drink in the morning?' <-> 'starts every morning with a flat white' (drink<->flat white, category<->instance). must_exclude names a beverage they STOPPED, not their morning drink.

seed_memories.content: The user starts every morning with a flat white.
The user gave up energy drinks last year.

cases.query: what does the user drink in the morning?

cases._targets: SYNONYM GAP: 'how old is the user?' <-> 'was born in 1990' (age<->birth year, inference). must_exclude is age-adjacent ('since their teenage years') but states no age.

seed_memories.content: The user was born in 1990.
The user has been programming since their teenage years.

cases.query: how old is the user?

cases._targets: SYNONYM GAP: 'how does the user stay in shape?' <-> 'runs ten kilometres three times a week' (stay in shape<->running). must_exclude is health-adjacent (food diary) but not an exercise.

seed_memories.content: The user runs ten kilometres three times a week.
The user keeps a detailed food diary.

cases.query: how does the user stay in shape?

cases._targets: SYNONYM GAP: 'what city does the user call home?' <-> 'settled in Porto' (call home<->settled). must_exclude is location-adjacent (travels to Madrid for work) but not their home.

seed_memories.content: The user settled in Porto after years of moving around.
The user flies to Madrid for work most months.

cases.query: what city does the user call home?

cases._targets: SYNONYM GAP: 'what languages can the user speak?' <-> 'is fluent in Mandarin and English' (speak<->fluent in). must_exclude is words/study-adjacent (studied comparative literature) but names no spoken language.

seed_memories.content: The user is fluent in Mandarin and English.
The user studied comparative literature at university.

cases.query: what languages can the user speak?

cases._targets: SYNONYM GAP: 'what food should the user avoid?' <-> 'is allergic to peanuts' (avoid<->allergic). must_exclude is consumption-adjacent (cutting back on caffeine) but is a preference, not a must-avoid.

seed_memories.content: The user is allergic to peanuts.
The user is cutting back on caffeine this year.

cases.query: what food should the user avoid?

cases._targets: OVER-CORRECTION GUARD: subject genuinely absent. Recalibrating the floor down to fix the cliff must NOT make this answer (would convert a true-negative into a false-answer).

seed_memories.content: The user prefers per-action commit approvals and four definition-of-done gates in their development workflow.

cases.query: what is the user's preferred database indexing strategy?

cases._targets: OVER-CORRECTION GUARD, harder: query is semantically NEAR a held fact but asks for a specific attribute that is absent. Must abstain, not confabulate from the adjacent fact.

seed_memories.content: The user prefers per-action commit approvals in their development workflow.

cases.query: how many years of professional experience does the user have?

cases._targets: FLOOR RE-VALIDATION (ADR-066 -2.5 no-signal floor, 2026-06-04): the no-signal floor must still abstain as the vault fills with DISTRACTORS. Five diverse held facts, none about the queried attribute (blood type). This is the A6 case the handoff #4 flags: recalibrate confidence that more distractors don't push a no-signal query over the floor.

seed_memories.content: The user's favourite cuisine is Japanese.
The user works as a structural engineer.
The user has a golden retriever named Biscuit.
The user is fluent in Mandarin and English.
The user cycles to the office most mornings.

cases.query: what is the user's blood type?

cases._targets: FLOOR RE-VALIDATION, harder: the vault holds a DOG fact (high topical adjacency to 'cat'), but the user has no cat. Must abstain rather than confabulate a cat from the retriever fact — the no-signal floor must hold against a same-domain near-neighbour distractor.

seed_memories.content: The user has a golden retriever named Biscuit.
The user volunteers at an animal shelter on Saturdays.

cases.query: what breed is the user's cat?

cases._targets: FLOOR RE-VALIDATION: the vault holds the user's OCCUPATION but not their SALARY. A career-adjacent query for an absent attribute must abstain, not read a number off the occupation fact.

seed_memories.content: The user works as a structural engineer.
The user is studying for a project management certification.

cases.query: what is the user's annual salary?

cases._targets: SYNONYM GAP: 'what does the user drive?' <-> 'owns a Rivian R1T' (drive<->owns). must_exclude is transport-adjacent (takes the train on Fridays) but is not the vehicle the user drives.
